title: aureolaria flava subsp. macrantha
intro: Aureolaria flava subsp. macrantha, also known as the large yellow rattle, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant that obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of surrounding plants. The medicinal properties of Aureolaria flava subsp. macrantha include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating respiratory issues and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, can be used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds.
